title: thebasics-handbook-title-tpa
text: TPA is request-based teleporting. The other player must accept before anyone moves, and some servers require a temporal gear in hand to send a request. TPA commands - /tpa <player>: ask to teleport to another player. - /tpahere <player>: ask another player to teleport to you. - /tpaccept [player]: accept an incoming request. The player name is optional when only one request exists. - /tpdeny [player]: deny an incoming request. - /tpalist: list incoming requests and remaining time when timeouts are enabled. - /tpallow on|off: allow or block receiving requests. - /tpacancel: cancel your outgoing request. - /cleartpa: clear all incoming requests to you. Temporal gear behavior When required, the requester must hold a temporal gear in either hand. The gear is consumed after the request passes validation. If the request is denied, cleared, cancelled, or times out, the gear is returned to inventory, hand, or dropped at the requester's feet if there is no room. Player stats Use /playerstats [player] or /pstats [player] to view your stats or another online player's stats when enabled. Servers can track deaths, player kills, NPC kills, block breaks, and distance travelled. Utilities Server save notifications can warn everyone when a save starts and finishes. Expect brief lag during saves. Staff/root users may also have /setdurability <durability>; use /setdurability 100% to fully repair the held durable item.
